claim: Code World Models trained on execution traces understand code semantics (cause/effect) better than syntax-only LLMs
actionable: Use CWM-style world models for agentic coding tasks where reasoning about code execution is critical

claim: Training on observation-action trajectories (variables, memory state) teaches models to manipulate code state, not just replicate syntax
actionable: When training coding models, include execution traces showing variable and memory changes to improve semantic understanding

claim: Agents trained with world models can plan and reason better than trial-and-error agents for bug fixing and feature addition
actionable: Use world models for agentic tasks requiring multi-step planning (bug fixing, refactoring) instead of pure trial-and-error

claim: World models enable backtracking behavior where agents detect mistakes and correct course mid-execution
actionable: Design agents with memory and backtracking support so they can revise failed branches instead of always moving forward
